input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI

**XAML — inline per-property binding:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome!"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page reacts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` takes `Light`, `Dark`, and an optional `Default` value. The framework swaps values automatically when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**Better at scale — use `AppThemeBinding` inside a `ResourceDictionary`:**

```xml
<!-- In App.xaml or a merged dictionary -->
<ResourceDictionary>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

Then reference with `{StaticResource PageBg}` so you only define colors once.

---

**C# equivalent — `SetAppTheme` extension method:**

```csharp
// In your page constructor or handler
BackgroundColor = new AppThemeBindingExtension
{
    Light = Colors.White,
    Dark  = Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")
}.ProvideValue(null) as Color; // one-shot, won't update live

// Preferred: use SetAppThemeColor (reactive, updates on theme change)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    targetLight: Colors.Black,
    targetDark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

myButton.SetAppThemeColor(
    Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    targetLight: Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    targetDark:  Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

**Key points:**

| Concern | How |
|---|---|
| Detect current theme | `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| React to changes in C# | Subscribe to `Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged` |
| Force a theme | `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` |
| XAML shorthand | `{AppThemeBinding Light=…, Dark=…}` on any bindable property |

The XAML `AppThemeBinding` approach is the cleanest — it's declarative, reactive, and requires zero code-behind.
